title: Make default preset startup persistent and host-ready
summary: 0.6.64 keeps the 0.6.63 plugin UI intact, but moves the default-preset startup state into plugin data and leaves the startup load probe armed until the attached player is actually ready.
changes: The chosen default preset id and Load Default at Start flag now persist through plugin data under Custom\\PluginData\\FrameAngel\\Player instead of only relying on scene-storable state.
The default-preset startup probe now keeps polling at a low cadence until the attached player selector is actually ready, instead of expiring after an early fixed timeout.
The visible plugin UI and player transport behavior stay unchanged from 0.6.63.
reasoning: The default-preset seam was close, but it depended on an early startup window and scene-local restore timing. This slice keeps the surface the same while making the startup behavior survive generated scenes and delayed host readiness.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Set Default Preset and Load Default at Start now survive across regenerated dev scenes.
A checked Load Default at Start toggle no longer silently misses just because the hosted player came up after the old probe window.
knownIssues: This slice does not change slideshow, sync cadence, right-stick volume, deferred gaze, or the current transport model.
The old internal shortcut actions remain registered for scene and control-surface compatibility.
verification: FASyncRuntime.PlayerPresets.cs now reads and writes preset startup preferences in Custom\\PluginData\\FrameAngel\\Player.
The startup default-preset probe in FASyncRuntime.PlayerPresets.cs no longer expires before hosted player readiness is available.
